title: Eureka Static Public Site Pack
heading: Eureka
summary: Eureka is a Python reference backend prototype for evidence-backed archive resolution, with a current usefulness wedge around old-platform-compatible software search and member-level discovery inside bundles.

sections.text: Current maturity: Eureka is not production. This generated static output is public-facing documentation only; it does not deploy a backend or run live searches.

panels.title: What It Is
paragraphs: A local-first reference backend that records sources, plans bounded archive/software queries, exposes result lanes, compatibility evidence, member records, and eval reports through CLI, HTTP API, and server-rendered web surfaces.

panels.title: What It Is Not
paragraphs: It is not broad live archive search, not a native GUI app, not an app-store product, and not a production hosted service.

panels.title: Current Demo Shape
paragraphs: The safe demo corpus is fixture-backed: synthetic fixtures, recorded GitHub release fixtures, Internet-Archive-shaped recorded fixtures, local bundle fixtures, and article/scan recorded fixtures.

links.text: Read the eval and audit status
